
### Linux memory allocation calls  {.smaller}


* <code>brk()</code> 
   * set ‘break’ at end of heap region
   * one big memory region for dynamic memory
   * used to be only way to allocate memory
   * minimum size of changes = 4KB (x86-64)
   * want larger changes for speed
* <code>mmap()</code>  
   * allocate new memory region
   * more complex OS bookkeeping than brk() 
      * adding to list of memory regions, not changing a size
   * minimum size = 4KB
   * want much larger allocations for speed


### malloc()/free()/etc. as memory partitioners  {.smaller}

* for "small" objects (less than kilobytes)
* malloc() allocates <i>big chunks of memory</i>
* then subdivides them on the fly
<hr class="vspace" />
* and  resizes (realloc()) if room
* and/or reuses after free'd
<hr class="vspace" />
* ... without contacting OS each time


### malloc() metadata?  {.smaller}


* malloc/free use metadata to...
<hr class="vspace" />
* track unused chunks of memory 
   * even after A=malloc(), B=malloc(), C=malloc(), free(B)
* [figure out how big allocation is when free() is called]{.fragment .custom .myem-only}
* [figure out if nearby blocks of memory are free when free() is called]{.fragment .custom .myem-only}
  *  merge multiple free regions together
  *  eventually free memory

### some metadata tracking strategies {.smaller}

* two common ideas for tracking metadata:
<hr class="vspace" />
* before 'arenas’ of allocations (example: jemalloc [Firefox; *BSD default?]) 
  * lookup for free() by rounding memory address
* [before each allocation/beginning of free blocks]{.fragment .custom .myem-only} (example: GNU libc malloc [Linux default; Windows default?])
  * lookup for free() by subtracting from pointer
  * use free space to hold metadata
  * probably [linked lists]{.fragment .custom .myem-only} or tree of free blocks
    * free blocks contain pointers for list or tree

### heap object metadata

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=30%}

```c
struct AllocInfo {
    bool free;
    int size;
    AllocInfo *prev;
    AllocInfo *next;
};
```

::::

:::: {.column}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/heapLayout.figure.svg){style="height: 500px; position: relative; top: -50px; overflow-y: visible"}

::::

:::::



### implementing free()

<pre><code>int free(void *object) {
  ...
  block_after = object + object_size;
  if (block_after-&gt;free) {
    /* unlink from list,
       prepare to merge with previous block */
    new_block-&gt;size += block_after-&gt;size;
    <span data-fragment-index="2" class="fragment custom myem-only">block_after-&gt;prev-&gt;next = block_after-&gt;next;</span>
    <span data-fragment-index="2" class="fragment custom myem-only">block_after-&gt;next-&gt;prev = block_after-&gt;prev;</span>
  }
  ...
}
</code></pre>
 

* [<em>arbitrary memory writes</em>]{.fragment fragment-index=2}
